title: Malty Crunchy Vietnamese Steak Cha-Gio
description: A rich and savory Vietnamese dish featuring crispy fried steak, served with a side of malty sauce and crunchy toasted rice. Perfect for hangover cures or weeknight dinners.
instructions: Cut the sirloin steak into 1-inch cubes and season with salt and pepper.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Dredge the beef cubes in the cornstarch, shaking off excess.
Fry the beef cubes until browned and cooked through, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the beef to a paper towel-lined plate and set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together the fish sauce, soy sauce, and sugar.
Add the garlic to the bowl and mix until well combined.
Coat the fried beef cubes in the fish sauce mixture, shaking off excess.
Place the coated beef cubes on a plate and set aside.
In a separate pan, heat a tablespoon of oil over medium heat.
Add the toasted rice to the pan and cook, stirring frequently, until crispy, about 5 minutes.
Add the malty sauce to the rice and stir to combine.
To assemble the cha-gio, place a portion of the crispy rice on a plate, followed by the fried beef cubes and a dollop of malty sauce.
Garnish with toasted rice and malty sauce, if desired.
